components.name: uracil
components.evidence: with 0.2 mM uracil, supplemented with 0.1% (w/v) lactose, maltose or lactate ... adding uracil compensates for intrinsic pyrimidine starvation of the MG1655 strain

components.name: D-lactate (sodium lactate)
components.evidence: supplemented with 0.1% (w/v) lactose, maltose or lactate and 0.001% (w/v) lactose in the limiting case

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: Growth was aerobic at 37 C (E. coli MG1655 derivatives grown at 37 C)

components.name: water (H2O)
components.evidence: aqueous M9 minimal medium

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (self-contained, fully specified in the paper's Methods)

sources_chased.what: Base M9 recipe / any cited base-medium reference
sources_chased.how: Read full text via EuropePMC fullTextXML and PMC HTML; the paper states all M9 macronutrient concentrations explicitly and cites NO base-medium reference

sources_chased.what: Any trace-element or vitamin stock solution
sources_chased.how: Verified against Methods (EuropePMC XML + PMC HTML): no trace-metal mix, no micronutrient solution, and no vitamins are added; classic M9 relies on reagent-impurity trace metals

media.notes: Paper gives a complete, self-contained M9 recipe with no cited base medium. No trace-element solution or vitamins are added. The Co2+/Cu2+/Fe2+/Fe3+/Mn2+/Zn2+ entries in current_components are metabolic-model exchange artifacts, not real medium ingredients, so they are omitted here. Lactate 0.1% (w/v) = 1 g/L -> ~11.1 mM (lactate MW 90.08). Growth aerobic at 37 C.

media.notes: Same self-contained M9 base as the lactate medium; carbon source is lactose 0.1% (w/v) = 1 g/L -> ~2.92 mM (lactose MW 342.3), with a 0.001% (w/v) limiting case also reported. No trace-element or vitamin solution added; model trace-metal exchanges are FBA artifacts, omitted. Growth aerobic at 37 C.
